components.description: Sizes, states, and styles — all designed. Pixel-perfect UI, ready to use.

categories.dataDisplay: Components for displaying and organizing data
categories.overlay: UI elements that layer on top of the page
categories.navigation: Navigation for page transitions and in-page movement
categories.charts: Visualize data with charts and metrics

components.quickStart: to install, then import any component to start building. TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, and Radix UI included.

install.description: How to install and use {name} UI components in your project.

tailwindRequired.title: Tailwind required:
tailwindRequired.body: Tailwind v3 or v4 is required. After installing, configure the imports below.

install.v4Note: The @source directive adds component Tailwind classes to the scan target. Adjust the path to match your project structure.
install.v3Note: The preset uses theme.extend, so your existing Tailwind classes are preserved. Semantic color opacity modifiers (bg-primary/50 etc.) are also supported in v3 via RGB channel variables.
install.bodyStyle: To apply design token background and text colors to the entire page, add classes to the body element.
install.nextjsNote: When using as a client component, add 'use client' at the top of your file.
install.fontsNote: Components use the token {code}. Configure fonts in Design Tokens > Installation > Fonts.
install.usageDesc: How to import and use components. Two import styles are supported: Namespace and Named.
install.namespaceNote: Import only the root component and access sub-components via dot notation.
install.namedExportNote: Traditional Named Exports are still supported. Existing code will continue to work.
install.standaloneNote: Standalone components (Button, Input, Badge, etc.) only need a single import — no Namespace required.
install.contentsDesc: Composed of two packages. Components and tokens can be managed separately.
install.tokensDesc: Design tokens — CSS variables, Tailwind presets, JS, CLI
install.customizeDesc: Changes to tokens are automatically reflected in component styles.

theming.description: {name} uses a CSS variable-based theme system with automatic Light / Dark switching and brand color customization.
theming.tokenChangeNote: Change a token in one place, and all referencing components update automatically.
theming.darkModeDesc: Switch themes and all components respond automatically.
theming.darkClassNote: Simply toggle the dark class on <html> and all CSS variables switch automatically.
theming.customizingDesc: Change the primary color and all components update automatically.
theming.customizeNote: Customize by overriding CSS variables or using Token Sync for batch changes.
theming.colorRefDesc: Semantic color variable reference. References switch automatically between Light / Dark themes.
theming.hoverActiveNote: * Each category also has hover and active variants available.

button.description: Buttons are the most fundamental interactive elements for users to perform actions. Trigger operations with clicks or taps — used for form submission, navigation, and more.

props.variant: Button style. Font weight: solid=600, outline/ghost/link=400
props.color: Color for solid variant (default=dark, primary=brand, secondary=secondary, destructive=error). Ignored for non-solid
props.fontWeight: Override font weight. Defaults to variant default (solid=600, outline/ghost/link=400). Priority: direct prop > ButtonGroup context > variant default
props.selected: Selected state (outline/ghost only). outline=bg-background-muted, ghost=font-semibold. No effect on other variants
props.pressEffect: Press effect (active:scale-pressed). Defaults to true. Set false to disable

solid.desc: Solid fill — the most visually prominent option

ghost.desc: Minimal — no background fill, ideal for toolbars

link.desc: Text link style — for inline actions

secondary.desc: Supplementary — pairs with a primary action

destructive.desc: Destructive — for deletes and irreversible actions

button.touchTargetNote: Touch target: WCAG 2.5.8 requires a minimum 24px touch area. Even the smallest xs (28px) meets the WCAG standard.

bestPractices.do3: Show loading state for async operations
bestPractices.dont1: Don't place multiple primary buttons side by side
bestPractices.dont4: Don't use destructive for safe operations

related.spinner: Used for loading display inside buttons.

code.tailwindDesc: How to apply styles for your environment

tips.title: Tips for using Button effectively
tips.variant: Set style with variant, override with className.
tips.loading: loading={true} automatically sets disabled state and shows a spinner.
tips.asChild: Use asChild to wrap other elements like Next.js Link.
tips.icons: Create icon buttons with leftIcon/rightIcon. Icon size adjusts automatically.

iconButton.description: IconButton is a square button that displays only an icon. Used in toolbars, navigation, and compact UIs.

props.color: Color for solid variant. Ignored for non-solid
props.pressEffect: Press effect (active:scale-pressed). Defaults to true. Set false to disable

solid.desc: Solid fill — the most visually prominent option

ghost.desc: Minimal — no background fill, ideal for toolbars

subtle.desc: Subtle — revealed on hover, great for headers and nav

secondary.desc: Supplementary — pairs with a primary action

destructive.desc: Destructive — for deletes and irreversible actions

iconButton.touchTargetNote: Touch target: WCAG 2.5.8 requires a minimum 24px touch area. Even the smallest xs (28px) meets the WCAG standard.

bestPractices.dont3: Don't overuse instead of text buttons
bestPractices.dont4: Don't use sizes that are too small

code.tailwindDesc: How to apply styles for your environment

tips.title: Tips for using IconButton effectively
tips.ariaLabel: Always specify aria-label for icon-only buttons. Required for screen readers.
tips.subtle: The subtle variant is ideal for auxiliary icons in headers and navigation.
tips.iconSize: Icon size auto-adjusts based on size. No need to set size classes on child <svg>.
tips.loading: The loading prop shows a spinner and automatically sets disabled state.

buttonGroup.description: ButtonGroup is a container component that visually groups related buttons. Used for toolbars, filters, action groups, and presenting multiple choices as a single unit.
buttonGroup.usageGuide: Specify variant and size on ButtonGroup, and they auto-inherit to child Button/IconButtons. Individual overrides are also possible.

props.radius: Child Button border radius (inherited via Context)
props.fontWeight: Child Button font weight (inherited via Context)
props.attached: Whether to visually join buttons together

ghost.desc: Minimal — ideal for compact UI and navigation

buttonGroup.sizesDesc: ButtonGroup inherits Button size via Context. Same size rules as Button apply.
buttonGroup.touchTargetNote: Touch target: WCAG 2.5.8 requires a minimum 24px touch area. Even the smallest xs (28px) meets the WCAG standard.

orientation.horizontalDesc: Arrange buttons horizontally. Ideal for toolbars and action groups.
orientation.verticalDesc: Arrange buttons vertically. Ideal for sidebars and dropdown menus.

customization.desc: All buttons use default values based on the design system spacing rules. Use the group's className to batch-customize all buttons.
customization.note: Default values are designed to maintain component consistency. Use customization when needed for UI implementation.

bestPractices.do2: Specify variant/size on ButtonGroup (inherits to children)
bestPractices.dont4: Don't mix destructive buttons with others

comparison.desc: They look similar but differ in purpose and behavior. Choose the appropriate component.
comparison.guideline: Selection guide: Use ToggleGroup when you want automatic state management. Use ButtonGroup when clicking triggers a specific action (API calls, etc.).

code.tailwindDesc: How to apply styles for your environment

tips.title: Tips for using ButtonGroup effectively
tips.attached: In attached mode, buttons connect as one control. Default is true.
tips.variant: Specifying variant on the group auto-applies to child buttons. outline and ghost are supported.
tips.orientation: orientation="vertical" switches to vertical layout. Ideal for menus and sidebars.

input.description: Input is a basic form element for collecting text data from users. Supports various text input types including email, password, and search.

props.focusRing: Show custom focus ring. Keyboard-only focus ring shown even when false
props.type: Input type (text, email, password, etc.). HTML default: text

fieldProps.error: Error state. Auto-propagates to Field.Error and Input
fieldProps.disabled: Disabled state. Auto-propagates to Field.Label and Input

labelProps.required: Show required mark (*) at end of label

default.desc: Standard border — for general form inputs

input.touchTargetNote: Touch target: WCAG 2.5.8 requires a minimum 24px touch area. Even the smallest xs (36px) meets the WCAG standard.
input.iosSafariNote: iOS Safari: Font size 16px+ prevents auto-zoom on iOS Safari. Sizes default and above use 16px font and 16px icons.
input.focusStatesDesc: focusRing: false (default): Border change only on click, focus ring auto-shown on keyboard Tab. focusRing: true: Always show focus ring.

bestPractices.do2: Use appropriate input type (email, tel, etc.)
bestPractices.dont1: Don't use placeholder as a label substitute
bestPractices.dont3: Ensure errors aren't indicated by color alone

code.tailwindDesc: How to apply styles for your environment

tips.title: Tips for using Input effectively
tips.icons: Create icon inputs with leftIcon/rightIcon. Icon container is pointer-events-none, so place clickable elements separately.
tips.filled: The filled variant is a borderless background style. Ideal for reducing visual noise in forms.
tips.focusRing: focusRing={false} hides the focus ring, showing only border changes.
tips.error: The error prop shows error state. Integrating with Field component also auto-displays error messages.

textarea.description: Textarea is a form element for entering multiple lines of text. Used for comments, descriptions, messages, and other long-form input.

props.size: Padding and font size (compact: for tight spaces)
props.focusRing: Show custom focus ring. Keyboard-only focus ring shown even when false

fieldProps.error: Error state. Auto-propagates to Field.Error and Textarea
fieldProps.disabled: Disabled state. Auto-propagates to Field.Label and Textarea

labelProps.required: Show required mark (*) at end of label

default.desc: Standard border — for general form inputs

textarea.sizesDesc: Textarea height is controlled by the rows attribute. The size property only adjusts padding and font size.

resize.vertical: Vertical only — the most common option
resize.both: Both directions — when full flexibility is needed

textarea.focusStatesDesc: focusRing: false (default): Border change only on click, focus ring auto-shown on keyboard Tab. focusRing: true: Always show focus ring.

bestPractices.do1: Set initial height with appropriate rows attribute
bestPractices.do2: Show counter when character limit exists
bestPractices.do3: Set resize="none" when resize is not needed
bestPractices.dont1: Don't use Textarea for short text (use Input)
bestPractices.dont3: Don't truncate without disclosing character limits upfront

code.tailwindDesc: How to apply styles for your environment

tips.title: Tips for using Textarea effectively
tips.resize: Control resize direction with the resize prop. Default is vertical. Set to none for fixed size.
tips.filled: Same filled variant as Input. Borderless background style.
tips.rows: Set initial rows with the rows prop. Combine with resize="none" for fixed height.
tips.error: The error prop shows error state. Can be integrated with the Field component.

select.description: Select is a form control for choosing a single value from a dropdown list. It features an accessible implementation with keyboard navigation and screen reader support, and also supports native form submission.

rootProps.size: Trigger height and font size. Auto-propagates to Content
rootProps.radius: Border radius size (6px). Applied directly to Trigger, auto-mapped to Content
rootProps.name: Name for form submission

basic.desc: The simplest select — a single-choice list

grouped.desc: Organize related items with groups and labels.

placeholder.desc: Show placeholder when nothing is selected.

flush.desc: Full-width items — no border radius, edge-to-edge

customization.desc: to customize the style of triggers and content.
customization.tip: Each sub-component accepts className, allowing you to override styles with Tailwind classes.

bestPractices.do1: Use when there are 5 or more options
bestPractices.do2: Use Group to classify when there are many options
bestPractices.do4: Set name and required in forms
bestPractices.dont1: Consider Radio for 3 or fewer options
bestPractices.dont3: Consider alternative UI for multi-select needs
bestPractices.dont4: Don't leave both placeholder and initial value empty

vsDropdown.desc: They look similar but serve different purposes. Choose the right component for your use case.
vsDropdown.guide: Select is appropriate when users choose a value to submit in a form. When clicking triggers an action (edit, delete, share, etc.), use

related.radio: Choose one from a few options

code.tailwindDesc: How to apply styles for your environment

tips.title: Tips for using Select effectively
tips.size: Set size on the Select root. It auto-propagates to both Trigger and Content, and Content item sizes are auto-mapped to 3 levels (sm/md/lg). Same 5-level scale as Input, so heights align in forms.
tips.placeholder: Use Select.Value's placeholder to show guidance text when nothing is selected. Once a value is selected, the placeholder is replaced with the selected value.
tips.position: "item-aligned" overlays the selected item on the trigger. "popper" displays below the trigger like Dropdown.
tips.nameRequired: Setting name and required on the Select root auto-generates a hidden input, sending the value through normal form submission.
tips.radius: Set radius on the Select root. Applied directly to Trigger and auto-mapped to Content (none–default→md, lg→lg, xl–full→xl). Item border radius is auto-calculated from Content proportionally. Same 9-level scale as Input.
tips.flush: Setting flush on Select.Content displays items full-width to Content edges. Content horizontal padding is removed and item padding is auto-adjusted. Same API as Dropdown.
tips.controlled: Use defaultValue for uncontrolled mode, value + onValueChange for controlled mode.

dropdown.description: Dropdown is a floating menu that appears when a trigger element is clicked. It supports action lists, checkboxes, radio selection, and submenus.

contentProps.radius: Content and item border radius (Item auto-calculated)

basic.desc: The simplest menu — a flat list of actions

group.desc: Organize related actions with labels and groups.

checkbox.desc: Toggle multiple items ON/OFF with checkboxes.

customization.desc: to customize the style of content and items.
customization.tip: Each sub-component accepts className, allowing you to override styles with Tailwind classes.

bestPractices.do1: Use when there are 3 or more actions
bestPractices.dont3: Don't nest submenus more than 2 levels

vsSelect.desc: They look similar but serve different purposes. Choose the right component for your use case.
vsSelect.guide: When users choose a value to submit in a form, use
vsSelect.guideSuffix: instead. Dropdown is appropriate when clicking triggers an action (edit, delete, share, etc.).

code.tailwindDesc: How to apply styles for your environment

tips.title: Tips for using Dropdown effectively
tips.asChild: Trigger renders as a Button by default. Use asChild to make any element the trigger.
tips.sideAlign: Control menu position. Default is side="bottom" and align="center". Position auto-adjusts at screen edges.
tips.onSelect: Each Item's onSelect is called on selection and automatically closes the menu. Use event.preventDefault() to prevent closing.
tips.radius: Choose Content border radius from "md" (default), "lg", or "xl". Item border radius is auto-calculated from Content proportionally (67–75%).
tips.inset: Adding inset to MenuItem, Label, or SubTrigger adds left padding matching CheckboxItem/RadioItem indicator area, aligning items vertically.

checkbox.description: Checkbox is a form control for toggling options on and off. Use it standalone or group multiple checkboxes together.

props.defaultChecked: Initial checked state for uncontrolled mode
props.label: Checkbox label text
props.name: Form name attribute

features.withFieldDesc: Combine with the Field component to automatically inherit disabled state.

bestPractices.do4: Combine with Field for required items
bestPractices.dont1: Don't use Checkbox for exclusive selection (use Radio)
bestPractices.dont4: Consider Select for too many options

code.tailwindDesc: How to apply styles for your environment

tips.title: Tips for using Checkbox effectively
tips.label: The label prop automatically associates the label. Recommended for accessibility.
tips.size: 3 sizes: sm(14px)/default(16px)/lg(20px). Label, icon, and gap auto-adjust.
tips.weight: Change border thickness with thin(1px)/bold(2px). Default is bold.
tips.indeterminate: For parent-child checkbox patterns, use Radix's indeterminate state.

radio.description: Radio is a form control for selecting exactly one option from multiple choices. Group with RadioGroup to provide exclusive selection.

groupProps.color: Border and dot color when checked
groupProps.name: Form name attribute

features.withFieldDesc: Combine with the Field component to automatically inherit disabled state.

bestPractices.do1: Use for exclusive selection (pick one only)
bestPractices.do4: Add clear labels to each item
bestPractices.dont2: Consider Select for too many options

code.tailwindDesc: How to apply styles for your environment

tips.title: Tips for using RadioGroup effectively
tips.orientation: Use orientation="horizontal" to switch to horizontal layout. Choose based on your form layout.
tips.label: Each RadioGroup.Item gets an automatically associated label via the label prop.
tips.size: 3 sizes: sm(14px)/default(16px)/lg(20px). Dot and label sizes auto-adjust.
tips.weight: Change border thickness with thin(1px)/bold(2px). Default is bold.

switch.description: Switch is a toggle control for instantly switching settings on and off. Use when changes take immediate effect.

props.label: Switch label text
props.color: Track and icon color when checked
props.checkedIcon: Icon displayed in thumb when checked
props.uncheckedIcon: Icon displayed in thumb when unchecked
props.name: Form name attribute

features.labelDesc: Use the label prop to attach a label to the switch.
features.labelPositionDesc: Change label placement with labelPosition prop. Default is end (right side).
features.iconsDesc: Display icons inside the thumb with checkedIcon / uncheckedIcon props. Auto-hidden at sm size.

bestPractices.do1: Use for settings with immediate effect
bestPractices.do3: Use for on/off toggles in settings
bestPractices.dont1: Use Checkbox when form submission is needed

code.tailwindDesc: How to apply styles for your environment

tips.title: Tips for using Switch effectively
tips.label: The label prop auto-associates the label. Recommended for accessibility.
tips.size: Track and thumb sizes managed via CSS variables. 3 sizes: sm/default/lg. Label font auto-adjusts: sm(12px)/default(14px)/lg(16px).
tips.labelPosition: 4 positions: start/end/top/bottom. For settings lists, start (label left, switch right) is common.
tips.sideLabels: Place labels on both sides of the switch. Useful for Off/On toggle displays.
tips.color: Set track background and icon color together when checked. 5 colors: default(dark)/primary/success/warning/error.
tips.icons: Display icons in thumb. Auto-hidden at sm size (12px thumb). Icon sizes: default(10px), lg(14px).
tips.controlled: Use defaultChecked for uncontrolled mode, checked + onCheckedChange for controlled mode.

toggle.description: Toggle is a button that switches between on/off states. Use standalone or group with ToggleGroup.

props.variant: Visual style. ghost/outline-ghost have no background change (icon color only)
props.iconOnly: Icon-only mode. Square layout with IconButton sizing rules
props.pressEffect: Press effect (active:scale-pressed). Defaults to true. Set false to disable

variants.default: Background changes on press — ideal for toolbars and compact UI
variants.outline: Bordered style — state is clearly visible
variants.ghost: No background change — state shown through icon color only, ideal for like buttons
variants.outlineGhost: Border + icon-only state — clear boundary with minimal visual change

bestPractices.dont1: Don't use for form items that need submission
bestPractices.dont2: Don't use icons with unclear state indication

code.tailwindDesc: How to apply styles for your environment

tips.title: Tips for using Toggle effectively
tips.controlled: Use pressed + onPressedChange for controlled mode. Use defaultPressed for uncontrolled.
tips.variant: default changes background, outline adds border, ghost changes text color only. Choose based on use case.
tips.iconOnly: Icon-only uses square layout. Always specify aria-label.
tips.dataState: For CSS customization, use data-[state=on] selector to change on-state styles.

toggleGroup.description: ToggleGroup groups multiple Toggles together to manage on/off state. Supports both single and multiple selection modes.

groupProps.defaultValue: Initial selected value in uncontrolled mode
groupProps.fontWeight: Font weight applied to all items. Can be overridden per item via fontWeight

itemProps.fontWeight: Overrides font weight for this item

types.singleDesc: Only one item can be selected at a time. Use for mutually exclusive choices like text alignment.
types.multipleDesc: Multiple items can be selected simultaneously. Use for options that can be applied at the same time, like text formatting.

variants.defaultDesc: No border — best for toolbars and compact UIs
variants.outlineDesc: Bordered and attached — use when grouping needs to be visually clear

states.defaultDesc: Normal state. Selected item is highlighted.
states.multipleSelectDesc: Multiple items can be selected simultaneously.

customization.descBefore: All items use default values based on the design system spacing rules. Use the group's
customization.descAfter: to customize all items at once.
customization.tip: Default values are designed to maintain component consistency. Use customization only when required for your UI implementation.

bestPractices.dont3: Don't use for navigation (→ use Tabs)
bestPractices.dont4: Don't use instead of Segmented for single selection

vsButtonGroup.intro: They look similar but differ in purpose and behavior. Choose the appropriate component.
vsButtonGroup.tip: Use ToggleGroup when you need to retain state (selected/unselected), and ButtonGroup when you want to trigger an action on click.

toggleGroup.tailwindDesc: How to apply styles for your environment

tips.type: `type="single"` for single selection, `type="multiple"` for multiple selection.
tips.attached: Items are automatically joined. Horizontally on left/right, vertically on top/bottom.
tips.variant: Two variants: `default` and `outline`. Unified across the entire group.
tips.orientation: `orientation="vertical"` switches to vertical layout. Great for sidebar navigation.

slider.description: Slider is an input control for selecting a value within a specified range. Supports both single value selection and range selection.

props.onValueChange: Callback when value changes (fires during drag)
props.color: Color of the range and thumb
props.minStepsBetweenThumbs: Minimum steps between thumbs in range mode
props.showTooltip: Tooltip display mode. auto shows on hover/drag
props.startContent: Content displayed on the left side of the slider (icon, label, etc.)
props.endContent: Content displayed on the right side of the slider
props.name: Form name attribute

features.rangeDesc: Pass two values to defaultValue to use as a range slider.
features.withValueDesc: Pattern for displaying the value in controlled mode.

customization.desc: Pass a ReactNode to startContent / endContent to freely customize styles.
customization.tip: startContent / endContent accept any ReactNode — strings, icons, or custom components. Font size auto-adjusts to match the Slider size.

do.2: Use range mode for range selection

dont.0: Use Input instead for precise value entry
dont.2: Don't use without showing the current value
dont.3: Avoid sliders that are too narrow on mobile

slider.tailwindDesc: Style application methods for your environment

tips.size: Thumb size is managed via CSS variables. Track thickness changes with h-1/h-1.5/h-2.
tips.step: Use the step prop to set the value increment. Combine with min/max to control the range.
tips.showTooltip: "auto" shows on hover/drag, "always" shows at all times. Use formatLabel to customize the display format.
tips.defaultValue: Use defaultValue for uncontrolled mode, and value + onValueChange for controlled mode.

segmented.description: Segmented is a pill-shaped control for exclusive single-selection. Ideal for filter and view switching.

props.defaultValue: Initial selected value in uncontrolled mode
props.variant: Visual style variant (default: iOS-style, outline: bordered, underline: Stripe-style, ghost: Notion-style)
props.fontWeight: Font weight applied to all items

itemProps.contentType: Content type (icon: square, icon-text: with gap, text: standard)

variants.defaultDesc: Muted container with selected item elevated by white background and shadow — iOS/Apple style
variants.outlineDesc: Bordered container with selected item on a grey background — clean border style
variants.underlineDesc: Selection shown by underline only — minimal tab style, similar to Stripe/GitHub
variants.ghostDesc: Transparent container with selected item showing a background — Notion/Figma style

customization.desc: All items use default values based on the design system spacing rules. Customize all items at once using className on the group.
customization.tip: Default values are designed to maintain component consistency. Use customization only when required for your UI.

do.1: Use for view switching within the same content area
do.2: Keep labels short and concise (1–2 words)
do.3: Best for filter and sort switching

dont.0: Don't use for 6 or more options
dont.1: Don't use for page navigation (→ Tabs)
dont.2: Don't use when multiple selection is needed (→ ToggleGroup)

vsTabs.intro: Similar appearance, but different purpose and structure. Choose the appropriate component.
vsTabs.tip: Use Segmented when changing the view within the same content area. Use Tabs when switching between different content panels.

segmented.tailwindDesc: Style application methods for your environment

tips.variant: `default` switches background, `outline` adds a border, `underline` uses underline style, `ghost` has no background. Choose based on use case.
tips.contentType: Specifying `contentType` as `icon`/`icon-text`/`text` auto-adjusts padding and icon size.
tips.fontWeight: `fontWeight="semibold"` makes the selected state text bold. Default is `normal`.
tips.controlled: Use `defaultValue` for uncontrolled mode, and `value` + `onValueChange` for controlled mode.

avatar.description: Avatar is a component that visually represents a user or entity. Supports image, initials, and icon fallbacks, with status indicators and group display.

props.name: Auto-generates initials and color from name. Overrides initials if children are provided
props.colorized: Auto-applies color only (initials manual). name takes precedence if provided
props.colorVariant: Color style: vivid (dark background + white text) / soft (light background + dark text)
props.max: Maximum number shown. Excess displayed as "+N"

features.fallbackDesc: When an image fails to load, initials or an icon are shown as a fallback.
features.groupDesc: Displays multiple avatars stacked together. Use max to limit the number shown.

customization.desc: Pass the name prop to auto-generate initials and a color.
customization.tip: The same name always produces the same color (deterministic hash). Pass children to override auto-generated initials.

do.1: Always pair Avatar.Image with a fallback
do.2: Use consistent sizes within a group
do.3: Use status only where it carries meaning

dont.0: Don't use image avatars without alt text
dont.1: Don't use Avatar.Image without a fallback
dont.2: Don't mix sizes within a group
dont.3: Don't use avatars for purely decorative purposes

a11y.statusDecorative: Status dot is decorative (supplement with text)

related.dropdownDesc: Show a menu on avatar click

tips.0: Provide avatar images at a small size. 2x resolution (e.g. 160px image for 80px display) is ideal for Retina screens.
tips.1: Always use Avatar.Image and Avatar.Fallback together. The fallback is shown when the image fails to load.
tips.2: Pass size to Avatar.Fallback as well to auto-scale the font size. Match it to Avatar's size.
tips.3desc: auto-generates initials (JD) and a color. Pass children to override the initials.
tips.4: Use the max prop on Avatar.Group to limit display count. The rest appear as "+N".
